articles.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
articles.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promise improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

articles.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100 million fundraise
articles.description: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.

articles.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
articles.description: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

articles.title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
articles.description: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.

articles.title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?
articles.description: The AI ROI debate has returned and the numbers are even bigger, as are, perhaps, the consequences.

articles.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
articles.description: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.

articles.title: Charles Hudson shares the common mistakes he’s seen after investing in 500+ startups
articles.description: In this week’s episode of Build Mode, Isabelle Johannessen talks with Precursor Ventures' Charles Hudson about the headwinds facing early-stage founders today and the most common mistakes founders should avoid in order to get funded.

articles.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
articles.description: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

articles.title: Slate Auto teams up with Crayola to color its EV truck
articles.description: Slate has an answer for owners who have always want to drive a truck with bright crayon colors.

articles.title: FanDuel sent a video from star athlete Bryce Harper to a customer with a gambling problem
articles.description: The video call highlights the murky relationship between professional athletes and gambling apps.

articles.title: Mercor is in talks for a $20B valuation
articles.description: A new $20 billion valuation would be a giant step up from the $10 billion valuation it reached in October.

articles.title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
articles.description: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.

articles.title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
articles.description: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

articles.title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
articles.description: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."

articles.title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
articles.description: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.

articles.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
articles.description: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

articles.title: Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
articles.description: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the sidelines.

articles.title: Block reaches $45M settlement with 46 states over Cash App fraud probe
articles.description: State attorneys general said they found that Block misled users by falsely advertising that Cash App provided bank-like protections, including advanced fraud detection.

articles.title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
articles.description: Claude’s new Reflect dashboard doesn’t just visualize how you use AI. It also subtly reinforces how much of your daily work now depends on Anthropic’s chatbot.

articles.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are bigger than the last 25 years of tech exits
articles.description: Three big AI IPOs are set to generate more value than all the U.S. VC-backed exits since 2000.

articles.title: Flores Hobbits' eating habits offer clues about their evolutionary past
articles.description: If Homo floresiensis wasn't a fire-using hunter, its origins could be different than we thought.

articles.title: Michigan's explosive outbreak of diarrheal parasite jumps to over 1,200 cases
articles.description: In neighboring Ohio, cases have reportedly reached over 500.

articles.title: OpenAI wants its new tool to do your work for you and with you
articles.description: Rebranded Codex promises independent workflows that can run "for hours if needed."

articles.title: Patch for Windows Defender 0-day could allow attackers to fill hard disk
articles.description: The feud between NightmareEclipse and Microsoft shows no signs of resolving soon.

articles.title: Allstate accuses Broadcom of auditing it because it quit VMware, CA
articles.description: Broadcom accuses Allstate of dodging VMware audits.

articles.title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
articles.description: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.

articles.title: Judge doesn't like Elon Musk settlement with SEC, but says court can't block it
articles.description: Judge reluctantly approves $1.5M settlement with SEC over Twitter stock violation.

articles.title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
articles.description: OpenAI may be sanctioned for hiding, deleting ChatGPT logs in NYT copyright fight.

articles.title: Surprised doctors find 10-inch worm in man's groin during elective surgery
articles.description: Oddly, it wasn't the first time this had happened to the man.

articles.title: Conspiracies and regrets abound in Dune: Part Three trailer
articles.description: "You promised me that you would never take power in your name."

articles.title: Ruf debuts new flat-eight engine at Goodwood
articles.description: The 4.8 L eight-cylinder generates more than 1,000 hp and 1,000 Nm, Ruf says.

articles.title: Free Waymo rides in California? You can thank a regulatory quirk.
articles.description: State agency's delay could mean free robotaxi rides in company’s new Ojai vehicle for a few months.

articles.title: The newest entrant in the military’s launch competition isn't actually a launch company
articles.description: "I think it's fair to say that Phase 3 did not contemplate this."

articles.title: Payloads used to dictate the terms of launch. That's finally changing.
articles.description: "The Starship Pez dispenser demonstrates very smart industrial design and scale."

articles.title: Suspecting AI cheating, Ivy League prof ordered an in-person final; scores fell 50%
articles.description: AI cheating leads to "a failed society," professor says.

articles.title: Lawsuit: Man used Grok to make 7K sex images of stepdaughter, then shot himself
articles.description: More young girls sue X over Grok CSAM; X accused of shielding child predators.

articles.title: Judge rejects Kalshi attempt to override New York state gambling laws
articles.description: Kalshi tried to ignore gambling laws on its prediction market, NY governor says.

articles.title: Google pays $250K for Linux vulnerability allowing guest VM escapes
articles.description: Both vulnerabilities allow untrusted users to gain root privileges.

articles.title: Aussie gov't tells volunteers to throw out thousands of functioning test routers
articles.description: But the devices could "easily be reflashed."

articles.title: TikTok users don't have as much agency over their FYPs as they think
articles.description: The "not interested" feature is your friend, but users must intentionally and constantly curate their FYPs

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **OpenAI发布GPT-5.6新模型系列，同时关闭Atlas项目**，但其AI浏览器野心仍在扩张；此外，《纽约时报》指控OpenAI在ChatGPT版权审判中隐瞒证据，引发法律争议。
2. **AI代理初创公司让自家代理完成1亿美元融资**，引发行业对AI自主执行商业任务能力的关注；巴黎AI语音公司Gradium获1亿美元种子轮融资，由英伟达支持。
3. **Meta推出Muse Spark 1.1加入AI编程竞争**；谷歌将披露AI生成的广告；Instagram用户可设置阻止Meta AI使用其照片训练模型。
4. **埃隆·马斯克称赞Mythos/Fable，并承诺不会“切断”Anthropic**；FanDuel向赌博问题用户发送明星运动员视频，引发伦理争议；Slate Auto与Crayola合作推出彩色电动卡车。
5. **Mercor估值谈判达200亿美元**；政府如何判定OpenAI前沿模型安全可发布成焦点；投资者Charles Hudson分享投资500+初创公司后的常见错误。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：OpenAI发布GPT-5.6并陷入版权诉讼，同时AI代理自主完成融资与估值飙升，显示AI技术正加速渗透商业与法律领域。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **Hugging Face多项产品更新**：包括推出面向智能体的数据工具（Data for Agents）、原生速度vLLM模型推理后端、一键部署至Amazon SageMaker Studio、模型在Foundry托管计算上运行、与SkyPilot合作的零出口存储跨云方案，以及机器人学习框架LeRobot v0.6.0和内核重大更新。
2. **OpenAI发布GPT-5.6**：该模型成为Microsoft 365 Copilot首选模型，同时推出面向雄心任务的ChatGPT合作伙伴模式、GPT-5.5生物漏洞赏金计划，并强调其前沿智能可随用户需求扩展。
3. **Google AI扩展Gemini API智能体功能**：新增后台任务、远程MCP（模型上下文协议）等能力，强化智能体在复杂工作流中的应用。
4. **OpenAI与政府及国家安全合作**：明确其与政府和国家安全机构合作的策略，同时发布代码评估中信号与噪声分离的方法论。

**最值得关注的发展**：OpenAI正式推出GPT-5.6并嵌入Microsoft 365 Copilot，标志着前沿大模型在办公场景的深度落地，同时Hugging Face密集发布多项基础设施级更新，加速AI开发与部署的标准化和效率提升。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **中国AI发展与产业创新**：多篇报道聚焦中国在人工智能领域的快速进展，包括美国诺贝尔奖得主将赴华领导AI研究所、中国AI创业生态催生新型企业家、以及中国考虑限制美国企业青睐的AI模型等，显示中美在AI领域的竞争与博弈日益激烈。

2. **中俄合作与地缘技术对抗**：泄露文件显示中俄可能制定针对“星链”的军事计划，同时两国联合在AI数据中心议题上推动争议讨论，凸显技术领域的地缘政治角力。

3. **中国核能建设与科技自主**：中国核能领域实现同时建造50座反应堆的能力，体现了重大基础设施与能源技术的自主突破；同时中国对Anthropic的AI编码工具发出“安全后门”警告，强化技术安全审查。

4. **中美科技竞争与市场动态**：报道指出中美在AI领域“黑暗中赛跑”，中国考虑对英伟达芯片实施新限制，以及非洲半数手机数据外流至中国等议题，反映全球科技供应链与数据主权的复杂博弈。

5. **社会文化现象与舆论驳斥**：中国漫展上展现“女巫”等小众题材，同时有文章驳斥“中国以百年计、美国以季度计”的刻板印象，体现文化多元性与对西方叙事的回应。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：中美在AI领域的竞争进入白热化阶段，从人才流动、技术限制到安全审查全面展开，同时中俄军事技术合作与核能自主突破进一步重塑全球科技与安全格局。
